skill.name: bruno-api
skill.description: Generate API endpoint documentation from Bruno files by mapping requests to Django REST Framework or Django Ninja implementations.
skill.summary: Document Bruno API requests with backend routes, schemas, auth, tests, and code references.
skill.author: DiversioTeam

security_audit.summary: AI review dismissed the static external-command and weak-cryptography alerts as false positives from Markdown prose and inline literals. The skill has low residual risk because it declares Bash and file editing tools and can write documentation to user-specified paths.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Command Findings Are Markdown Literals
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged Markdown inline code and command-like documentation as Ruby shell execution. These lines describe file names, headings, CLI flags, or output sections and are not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown skill instructions, and the flagged backticks mark literal examples or labels. I found no Ruby source, script file, or shell expansion syntax at these locations.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Prose Substring Matches
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography alerts are false positives. The flagged lines contain description text, a section title, and compatibility prose, not cryptographic functions or algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows ordinary prose and frontmatter, with no hash, cipher, password, or transport-security implementation. The matches appear to come from substrings inside words.

low_findings.title: Broad Local Tool Permissions Require Normal User Caution
low_findings.description: The skill declares Bash and file editing tools and can write generated documentation to a user-provided output path. This is legitimate for documentation generation but should remain limited to trusted repositories.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The tool declarations and output-writing workflow are explicit. I found no malicious command, but the capability can modify local files when the user asks for output writing.

content.value_statement: API teams need endpoint docs that match the real backend implementation. This skill turns Bruno requests into Django-focused documentation with auth, schemas, tests, and code references.
actual_capabilities: Parses Bruno files for method, URL, headers, query parameters, path parameters, and body hints.
Maps API paths to Django REST Framework or Django Ninja route implementations.
Documents authentication, permissions, tenant scoping, serializers, schemas, and validation behavior.
Generates endpoint markdown with implementation references, business logic notes, and testing guidance.
Supports single-file analysis, directory scans, dry-run planning, and output-file workflows.
limitations: Requires access to the Bruno files and the matching Django backend repository.
May mark sections as uncertain when routes, serializers, or response shapes cannot be located.
Does not execute API requests or prove runtime behavior by itself.
Backend conventions are tailored to Django REST Framework, Django Ninja, and Diversio-style layouts.

use_cases.title: Create Endpoint Reference Docs
use_cases.description: Generate a complete endpoint guide from one Bruno request and its Django implementation.

use_cases.title: Audit Tenant and Permission Behavior
use_cases.description: Trace authentication, permission classes, and tenant filters before documenting an internal API.

use_cases.title: Prepare Frontend Integration Notes
use_cases.description: Summarize request fields, response behavior, errors, and testing notes for client implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Document One Bruno File
prompt_templates.prompt: Use bruno-api to document this Bruno file: path/to/request.bru. Include auth, request fields, response behavior, and backend references.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Before Full Documentation
prompt_templates.scenario: Dry-run review before deeper repository search.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use bruno-api with --dry-run for path/to/request.bru. Show the endpoint summary and where you will search in the Django codebase.

prompt_templates.title: Scan a Bruno Directory
prompt_templates.prompt: Use bruno-api with --scan path/to/bruno-folder. Generate grouped documentation for every Bruno request you can map to backend code.

prompt_templates.title: Write Complete Docs to a Path
prompt_templates.prompt: Use bruno-api with --scan path/to/bruno-folder and --output docs/api-endpoints.md. Include uncertainties with severity tags and line references.

output_examples.input: A Bruno file for a Django REST Framework list endpoint.
output_examples.output: A markdown endpoint page with method, path, auth, permissions, request parameters, response summary, implementation references, and test notes.

output_examples.input: A directory containing several Bruno requests for one product area.
output_examples.output: Grouped endpoint documentation with a table of contents, one section per request, and notes for unresolved backend mappings.

output_examples.input: A dry-run request for an endpoint with unclear routing.
output_examples.output: A short analysis plan listing likely route files, search terms, expected documentation sections, and blocking unknowns.

best_practices: Run a dry run first when the repository layout is unfamiliar.
Provide both Bruno files and the Django backend in the same workspace.
Review severity tags before publishing generated documentation.
anti_patterns: Do not use it on repositories that you do not trust.
Do not treat inferred response shapes as verified production contracts.
Do not write output over source files or generated artifacts without review.

faq.question: What input does this skill need?
faq.answer: It needs one Bruno file or a directory of Bruno files, plus access to the matching Django backend code.

faq.question: Does it support both DRF and Django Ninja?
faq.answer: Yes. The instructions cover Django REST Framework views and serializers, plus Django Ninja routers and schemas.

faq.question: Can it scan many Bruno files at once?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the scan mode to process all Bruno files under a directory.

faq.question: Can it write documentation to a file?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide an output path when you want generated markdown written to disk.

faq.question: Will it call the API endpoint?
faq.answer: No. It documents the endpoint from Bruno request data and backend source code inspection.

faq.question: How does it handle missing implementation details?
faq.answer: It marks blocking gaps or uncertain details with severity tags so reviewers can resolve them.
